msgs.text:
Turn 3 — Stage B: make Γ_A an exact-image finite-quotient machine The next move should be structural, not another local proof sprint. Stage A’s Gen / GExpr / powOmega2 / twoCore / Admissible layer should now feed a single Stage B object:Γ_A is the inverse limit of all admissible finite marked quotients, and every finite continuous map out of Γ_A is controlled by its exact image.This is the Lean version of the manuscript’s Section 2: admissible finite quotients are generated marked finite groups satisfying the two relators and the pro-2 wild-normal-closure condition; Lemma 2.1 gives finite subdirect closure; Lemma 2.2 gives cofinality; Proposition 2.3 gives finite epimorphism semantics; Lemma 2.5 is the reconstruction criterion from finite surjection counts.The key refinement for Turn 3 is: prove exact-image semantics before epimorphism semantics. The manuscript explicitly warns that the non-surjective ambient statement is false: a non-surjective map into a finite ambient group only puts the wild subgroup inside the 2-core of the image, not necessarily inside the 2-core of the ambient group.This exact-image theorem is the structural pattern that will later feed boundary-framed targets, exact-image strata, proper-image subtraction, and Fourier recursion.

1. Replace “all finite groups” by a small skeletal index Do not make AdmissibleIndex range over arbitrary finite types. That will create universe and smallness pain when forming a category-theoretic limit. Use a skeleton on Fin n, with transport lemmas for arbitrary finite groups.

Schematic Lean:lean namespace Q2Presentation structure AdmissibleIndex where n : ℕ hn : Fact (0 < n)groupFin : Group (Fin n)q : Gen → Fin n admissible : by letI := groupFin exact Admissible q attribute [instance] AdmissibleIndex.hn attribute [instance] AdmissibleIndex.groupFin structure AdmissibleHom (A B : AdmissibleIndex) where toMonoidHom : (Fin A.n) →* (Fin B.n)map_q : ∀ a : Gen, toMonoidHom (A.q a) = B.q a instance : Category AdmissibleIndex where Hom A B := AdmissibleHom A B id A := ...comp f g := ...end Q2Presentation The important lemma is that morphisms are unique, because every object is generated by its marking:lean theorem admissibleHom_ext {A B : AdmissibleIndex} (f g : A ⟶ B) :f = g := by -- both maps agree on A.q; A.q generates A...This means the index category has no nontrivial parallel-arrow coherence problem. Cofilteredness reduces to finite subdirect closure, not to a separate equalizer construction. This is exactly where Turn 2’s admissible_subdirect theorem pays off.

Transport layer:lean def transportMarking {G H : Type*} [Group G] [Group H](e : G ≃* H) (q : Marking G) : Marking H :=fun a => e (q a)theorem admissible_transport_iff {G H : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] [Group H] [Fintype H](e : G ≃* H) (q : Marking G) :Admissible (transportMarking e q) ↔ Admissible q := ...noncomputable def skeletalize (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G] [Nonempty G](q : Marking G) (h : Admissible q) :AdmissibleIndex := ...This keeps the limit small while preserving a clean user-facing theorem for arbitrary finite groups.

2. Cofilteredness comes from one uniform subdirect lemma

Define the product/subdirect object once:lean noncomputable def subdirectObj (A B : AdmissibleIndex) : AdmissibleIndex :=-- image/closure of diagonal marking in (Fin A.n) × (Fin B.n),-- transported back to Fin(card ...)...def subdirectFst (A B : AdmissibleIndex) : subdirectObj A B ⟶ A := ...def subdirectSnd (A B : AdmissibleIndex) : subdirectObj A B ⟶ B := ...theorem admissibleIndex_isCofiltered :IsCofiltered AdmissibleIndex := ...The proof should not unfold the wild word. It uses only:lean GExpr.eval_pi admissible_subdirect admissible_transport_iff admissibleHom_ext This closes all finite intersections of kernels at once. It is the Lean mirror of Lemma 2.1 and the engine behind Lemma 2.2’s cofinality argument.3. Define Γ_A as the inverse limit and define the universal generatorslean noncomputable def admissibleDiagram :AdmissibleIndex ⥤ ProfiniteGrp where obj A := ProfiniteGrp.of (Fin A.n)map f := ProfiniteGrp.ofHom f.toMonoidHom map_id := ...map_comp := ...noncomputable def GammaA : ProfiniteGrp :=limit admissibleDiagram The universal generators are the compatible families of marked generators:lean noncomputable def gammaGen (a : Gen) : GammaA :=-- coordinate at A is A.q a...theorem gammaGen_eval_projection (A : AdmissibleIndex) (a : Gen) :admissibleDiagram.π.app A (gammaGen a) = A.q a := ...Then prove the relators in Γ_A by coordinatewise extensionality:lean theorem GammaA_tameRel :GExpr.eval Q2Word.tameRelator gammaGen = 1 := ...theorem GammaA_wildRel :GExpr.eval Q2Word.wildRelator gammaGen = 1 := ...The finite-word layer from Turn 2 is crucial here: every relator-preservation proof is just GExpr.eval_map plus projection extensionality, not a hand rewrite of h0. The manuscript’s actual relator and auxiliary words are fixed in Theorem 1.2, and Appendix C’s serialized integer is explicitly only for independent finite verification, not a proof dependency.

4. Prove topological generation by finite-coordinate approximation Do not prove generation by abstract free-profinite arguments. Use the concrete inverse-limit model.lean theorem gammaGen_topologically_generate :topologicalClosure (Subgroup.closure (Set.range gammaGen : Set GammaA)) = ⊤ := ...

Proof pattern:For any x : GammaA and any basic open neighborhood of x, only finitely many coordinates are constrained. Take the subdirect object over those finitely many coordinates. Since that finite subdirect object is generated by its marked tuple, choose a finite group word in Gen matching x on those coordinates. Evaluating the same word in GammaA gives an element of the abstract subgroup generated by gammaGen lying in the chosen neighborhood. Hence the generated subgroup is dense.This lemma is more valuable than it looks: it will later give finite determination of maps, uniqueness of maps out of Γ_A from generator values, and topological finite generation.

5. The finite-factorization theorem is the Stage B load-bearing API The central topological theorem for this stage should be general, not Γ_A-specific:lean theorem finiteMap_from_limit_depends_on_finite_coordinates {ι : Type*} [Category ι](D : ι ⥤ ProfiniteGrp)[∀ i, Fintype (D.obj i)](H : Type*) [Group H] [Fintype H](φ : limit D ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) :∃ (s : Finset ι) (ψ : finiteSubdiagramImage D s ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H),φ = ψ ≫ finiteSubdiagramProjection D s := ...

For the actual candidate, specialize it to:lean theorem GammaA_map_to_finite_factors (H : Type*) [Group H] [Fintype H](φ : GammaA ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) :∃ (A : AdmissibleIndex) (ψ : ProfiniteGrp.of (Fin A.n) ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H),φ = admissibleDiagram.π.app A ≫ ψ := ...The specialization uses cofiltered subdirect closure: a finite set of coordinates is replaced by one admissible subdirect object. This is the clean formal substitute for the manuscript’s compactness-and-finite-intersection argument in Lemma 2.2.

6. Prove exact-image semantics first For any finite ambient target and any continuous homomorphism, define its exact image as a finite subgroup with the induced marking:lean def exactImageSubgroup {H : Type*} [Group H](φ : GammaA ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) : Subgroup H :=MonoidHom.range φ.toMonoidHom noncomputable def exactImageMarking {H : Type*} [Group H](φ : GammaA ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) :Marking (exactImageSubgroup φ) :=fun a => ⟨φ (gammaGen a), by exact ⟨gammaGen a, rfl⟩⟩

Main theorem:lean theorem GammaA_hom_exactImage_admissible {H : Type*} [Group H] [Fintype H](φ : GammaA ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) :Admissible (exactImageMarking φ) := ...Proof: factor φ through an admissible finite stage A; the exact image is a quotient of A; admissible_map_surjective transports admissibility to the exact image.This theorem avoids the false ambient statement and gives the exact-image object needed later for boundary-framed induction.

7. Epimorphism semantics becomes a corollary Now the manuscript’s Proposition 2.3 becomes almost formal:lean structure SurjContHom (P Q : ProfiniteGrp) where hom : P ⟶ Q surj : Function.Surjective hom theorem GammaA_epi_equiv_admissible (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G] [Nonempty G] :SurjContHom GammaA (ProfiniteGrp.of G) ≃ { q : Marking G //Admissible q } := ...

Forward map:lean f ↦ fun a => f.hom (gammaGen a)Admissibility follows because the exact image is all of G.Backward map: skeletalize (G, q) as an admissible index object and take the corresponding projection from the limit; it is surjective because its image contains the generating tuple.Uniqueness of the equivalence uses gammaGen_topologically_generate: two continuous maps from Γ_A to a finite Hausdorff group are equal if they agree on gammaGen.This is the Lean form of Proposition 2.3: finite epimorphisms from Γ_A are exactly generating quadruples satisfying the two relations and the x0,x1 ∈ O₂(G) condition.

8. Reconstruction should be split into three reusable lemmas Do not try to prove Lemma 2.5 as a monolith. Split it:lean theorem tfg_hom_to_finite_finite (P : ProfiniteGrp) (S : Finset P)(hS : topologicalClosure (Subgroup.closure (S : Set P)) = ⊤)(H : Type*) [Group H] [Fintype H] :Fintype (P ⟶ ProfiniteGrp.of H) := ...theorem profinite_epimorphism_from_compatible_finite_quotients (P Q : ProfiniteGrp)(system : CofinalFiniteQuotientSystem P)(hNonempty :∀ i, Nonempty (SurjContHom Q (system.obj i)))(hCompatFinite :-- finite intersection property for compatible choices True) :Nonempty (SurjContHom Q P) := ...theorem tfg_profinite_hopfian (P : ProfiniteGrp)(hP : TopologicallyFinitelyGenerated P) :∀ f : SurjContEnd P, Function.Bijective f.hom := ...

Then assemble:lean theorem profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts (P Q : ProfiniteGrp)(hP : TopologicallyFinitelyGenerated P)(h :∀ (G : Type*) [Group G] [Fintype G] [Nonempty G],Fintype.card (SurjContHom P (ProfiniteGrp.of G)) =Fintype.card (SurjContHom Q (ProfiniteGrp.of G))) :Nonempty (P ≅ Q) := ...The manuscript’s reconstruction proof builds epimorphisms in both directions from compatible finite quotients and then uses Hopficity for the finitely generated profinite group.This should be formalized as a reusable Profinite/Reconstruction.lean, because it will be invoked only once at the end but is mathematically independent of the Q₂ word.

9. Turn 3 work packets for the coding agent

Give the coding agent narrow tasks only:

B1 — skeletal transport Implement AdmissibleIndex over Fin n. Prove admissible_transport_iff under MulEquiv.Do not define GammaA. Do not import ProfiniteGrp.

B2 — category and cofilteredness Define generator-preserving morphisms between AdmissibleIndex objects.Prove hom_ext and construct binary subdirect objects using the existing admissible_subdirect theorem.Return only Lean code for the category/cofiltered file.

B3 — inverse limit and generators Define admissibleDiagram and GammaA as its limit in ProfiniteGrp.Define gammaGen and prove projection evaluation, tame relator, wild relator, and dense generation.Do not prove epimorphism semantics yet.

B4 — finite-factorization Find/prove the theorem that a continuous map from an inverse limit of finite discrete profinite groups to a finite group factors through finitely many coordinates.Specialize it to GammaA and cofiltered subdirect coordinates.

B5 — exact-image semantics Define exactImageSubgroup and exactImageMarking for maps GammaA → finite H.Prove GammaA_hom_exactImage_admissible.Then prove GammaA_epi_equiv_admissible as a corollary.

B6 — reconstruction Split profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts into finite hom-count, compatible quotient epimorphism construction, and Hopfian lemmas.Use only ProfiniteGrp and finite quotient APIs. Do not import Q2Presentation words.10. Remote compile matrix

Use the 100 cores like this:15 jobs Admissible transport across MulEquiv and Fin skeleton 15 jobs category instance, hom_ext, surjectivity of marked morphisms 20 jobs binary and finite subdirect construction, cofilteredness 20 jobs finite-map factorization from inverse limits 15 jobs gammaGen dense generation and map extensionality 10 jobs exact-image and epi equivalence 5 jobs reconstruction theorem-name probes and import minimization

Every job should emit:lean

#print axioms theorem_name No theorem enters main-clean if it depends on a custom axiom or sorry. The prior plan already set this as the acceptance standard, and it is especially important here because Stage B creates the object that all later stages map out of.

11. Turn 3 completion criterion

Stage B is stable when these compile:lean

#check Q2Presentation.AdmissibleIndex

#check Q2Presentation.admissibleIndex_isCofiltered

#check Q2Presentation.admissibleDiagram

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA

#check Q2Presentation.gammaGen

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA_tameRel

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA_wildRel

#check Q2Presentation.gammaGen_topologically_generate

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA_map_to_finite_factors

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA_hom_exactImage_admissible

#check Q2Presentation.GammaA_epi_equiv_admissible

#check Q2Presentation.profinite_reconstruction_of_surj_counts At that point the outer shell of the proof is real: Γ_A exists as a profinite group, its finite epimorphisms are exactly the admissible quadruples, and the final theorem has a reconstruction endpoint. The next mathematical stage can then start the tame/pro-2 boundary comparison, where the full integral unramified marking becomes load-bearing. The manuscript stresses that this full ℤ₂ marking, not merely mod 2 data, is needed for the common boundary and terminal pro-2 comparison.

ESTABLISHED: Stage B should be implemented as a small skeletal admissible-quotient inverse system; the central structural theorem is exact-image admissibility for every finite map out of Γ_A, with epimorphism semantics as a corollary.

GAP: The two topological API risks are finite-factorization from profinite inverse limits to finite targets and the one-sided profinite reconstruction/Hopfian package. No Lean compile success is claimed here.

NEXT: Dispatch B1–B3 immediately while probing B4’s existing ProfiniteGrp limit API; do not enter local Q₂/Demushkin/Tate theory until GammaA_hom_exactImage_admissible and GammaA_epi_equiv_admissible are clean.[tag: q2-stageB-exact-image-inverse-limit]
